name: Abundance Theorem

phase_0.boundary_condition: The closed set of all possible allocations and their justifications

VINIT.justification: Before any resource is named or claimed, the domain exists as pure potentiality—void of distribution, prior to scarcity

TANCH.justification: The hermetic seal: total abundance is constant; nothing enters or leaves the system. The boundary that contains all valid allocations

AFWD.justification: Forward morphism: the directed transformation from scarcity-assumption toward abundance-demonstration. Ascent from doubt to certainty

AREV.justification: Reverse morphism: descent into the negation—the hypothesis that resources are insufficient. Contravariant to the proof

CLINK.justification: Composition of sufficiency checks: each allocation step chains into the next, building the proof inductively

IMSCRIB.justification: Identity: the theorem recognizes itself. The proof that proves itself—abundance is its own witness

FSPLIT.justification: Splits the domain into two exhaustive branches: (T) sufficiency holds for all allocations, (F) scarcity exists somewhere

FFUSE.justification: Reconstitutes the original question: if both branches are examined and one is proven false, the other must hold universally. Recovers the theorem

EVALT.justification: Affirmative evaluation: the T-branch confirms that for every agent and every need, allocation is possible without depletion

EVALF.justification: Negative evaluation: the F-branch is shown to be impossible—no counterexample exists where resources fail

ENGAGR.justification: Both states held simultaneously without resolution: the theorem admits that scarcity is real locally but abundance is real globally—a paradice of perspective

IFIX.justification: Permanent, append-only fixation: once the abundance theorem is proven and written, it cannot be undone. It becomes canonical truth

phase_2.split_input: The question: 'Is abundance possible for all?'
phase_2.fuse_result: The theorem: 'Abundance is possible for all' (recovered from proof that F-branch is impossible)

phase_3.void_description: 00 — Uninitialized: before the theorem is stated, no allocation scheme exists, no scarcity or sufficiency is defined
phase_3.true_description: 01 — Affirmative: sufficiency is proven; every agent can be satisfied without depletion; abundance holds
phase_3.false_description: 10 — Negative: scarcity is assumed; the F-branch is explored and shown to lead to contradiction
phase_3.both_description: 11 — Paradice: abundance and scarcity coexist—locally scarce (individual perspective) yet globally abundant (systemic perspective); both held without resolution

steps.domain_action: Unallocated potential — the domain begins in void, before any resource is named or distributed
Conservation law — the boundary is sealed; total abundance is constant and finite
Sufficiency proof begins — forward morphism toward the demonstration that all needs can be met
Bifurcation into cases — split the question into (T) 'sufficiency holds for all' and (F) 'scarcity exists'
T-branch anchored — affirmative evaluation: assume sufficiency and explore its consequences
Recursive verification on T-branch — chain each allocation step; verify no depletion occurs
Self-consistency on T-branch — the proof recognizes itself; sufficiency is self-witnessing
F-branch anchored — negative evaluation: assume scarcity and seek a counterexample
Scarcity assumption on F-branch — reverse morphism; descent into the negation
Recursive verification on F-branch — attempt to construct an allocation that fails
Paradice emerges — both branches held simultaneously; scarcity is real locally, abundance real globally
Unification of cases — reconstitute the original question; F-branch is shown impossible, T-branch is proven
Theorem statement recorded — permanent fixation: 'Abundance is possible for all' becomes canonical truth
Self-reference closes the loop — the theorem proves itself; abundance is its own witness and justification

phase_5.compiler_frontend: Logical deduction engine — translates the abundance hypothesis into formal allocation schemes and sufficiency proofs
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: Proof propagation — each verified allocation step communicates its success to the next, building consensus across all branches
phase_5.memory_mechanism: Canonical store of allocations — each valid distribution is recorded; the set of all feasible allocations is the memory
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: Exhaustive case analysis — determines the order of branch exploration; T-branch first (sufficiency), then F-branch (scarcity refutation)
phase_5.alfs_store: The conservation law — the bootstrap store; total abundance is the reference constant from which all allocations are derived

phase_6.cycle_cost: Cognitive effort — the cost of exploring both branches (sufficiency and scarcity) exhaustively
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: Uncertainty: the question 'Is abundance possible?' is unresolved; both T and F states are equiprobable
phase_6.post_cycle_state: Certainty: the theorem is proven; T-state (sufficiency) is established; F-state (scarcity) is refuted
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — The cycle is entropy-conservative because the proof is reversible: if you doubt the theorem, you can re-traverse both branches and recover the same conclusion. The information content is preserved; only the epistemic state changes from uncertainty to certainty, not the underlying abundance itself

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → TANCH → AFWD → FSPLIT → EVALT → CLINK → IMSCRIB → EVALF → AREV → CLINK → ENGAGR → FFUSE → IFIX → IMSCRIB
-- Class: Abundance Theorem
-- Fingerprint: sig=(8,2,3,1)
--   self_ref=False | frobenius_order=1
--   dialetheia_complete=True | period=14
-- Expected tier: O₂
-- FSPLIT/FFUSE pairs: [(3, 11)]

import Imscribing.IGMorphism
import Imscribing.IGFunctor

namespace Imscribing
open Primitives Frobenius IGProtocol
open Dimensionality Topology Relational Polarity Grammar
     Fidelity KineticChar Granularity Criticality Protection Stoichiometry Chirality

-- ── Token → IG field mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────
--   [0] VINIT     dim    := 𐑼               𐑼 → 𐑡  | initial object — ground of distinction
--   [1] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑼 → 𐑾  | terminal object — connectivity boundary
--   [2] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑡 → 𐑚  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [3] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [4] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [5] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑚 → 𐑙  | composition — regime coherence
--   [6] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑚 → 𐑙  | identity — self-imscription
--   [7] EVALF     chir   := 𐑖               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [8] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑚 → 𐑙  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [9] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑚 → 𐑙  | composition — regime coherence
--   [10] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑚 → 𐑙  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [11] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑭  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [12] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑙 → 𐑠  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [13] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑭 → 𐑼  | identity — self-imscription

-- ── Main IGProtocol term ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

noncomputable def abundance_theorem_protocol : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑠 :=
  .withGram 𐑠 <|
  -- Seq chain:
  (.arrow 𐑼 𐑼 𐑡)  -- [0] VINIT | dim := 𐑼 | initial object — ground of distinction (Unallocated potential — the domain begins in void, before any resource is nam...)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑼 𐑾)  -- [1] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (Conservation law — the boundary is sealed; total abundance is constant and fi...)
  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑡 𐑚)  -- [2] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (Sufficiency proof begins — forward morphism toward the demonstration that all...)
  -- FSPLIT [3] (gran := 𐑚) (Bifurcation into cases — split the question into (T) 'sufficiency holds for all' and (F) 'scarcity exists') / FFUSE [11] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (3 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [4] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (T-branch anchored — affirmative evaluation: assume sufficiency and explore it...)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑱 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [5] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (Recursive verification on T-branch — chain each allocation step; verify no de...)
        (.arrow 𐑠 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [6] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (Self-consistency on T-branch — the proof recognizes itself; sufficiency is se...)
      -- F-branch (4 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑖 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [7] EVALF | chir := 𐑖 | evaluate-false — chirality check (F-branch anchored — negative evaluation: assume scarcity and seek a counterex...)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑗 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [8] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (Scarcity assumption on F-branch — reverse morphism; descent into the negation)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑱 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [9] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (Recursive verification on F-branch — attempt to construct an allocation that ...)
        (.arrow 𐑳 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [10] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (Paradice emerges — both branches held simultaneously; scarcity is real locall...))
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [11]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑭)  -- [11] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (Unification of cases — reconstitute the original question; F-branch is shown impossible, T-branch is proven)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑙 𐑠)  -- [12] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (Theorem statement recorded — permanent fixation: 'Abundance is possible for a...)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑭 𐑼)  -- [13] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (Self-reference closes the loop — the theorem proves itself; abundance is its ...)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def abundance_theorem_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑠 :=
  (abundance_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm
noncomputable def abundance_theorem_false_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑠 :=
  (abundance_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALF

-- ── Verification theorems ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

theorem abundance_theorem_tier : TierFunctor.obj 𐑼 = .O₂ := by decide

-- Frobenius (split → fuse): μ∘δ = id on .prod branch
-- Proof: apply igFrobAlg_self_fusion; exact mu_delta_A_id
-- (requires mu_delta_A_id from IGFunctor library)

end Imscribing
